summary: Large gm_1832 decompilation PR for game-mode code, replacing many placeholder stubs and UNK prototypes with C implementations, typed signatures, local data-layout structs, and shared game-mode types. The author explicitly noted many functions were fuzzy rather than 100% matches, but considered the work an improvement; one broken function was attributed to data/offset issues expected to resolve when linking.

key_files.role: Main decompilation body; added roughly 2131 lines and replaced many placeholder /// #fn_8018xxxx stubs with C implementations and supporting static data/types.
Header cleanup; replaced multiple UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete return types and parameter types.
Added shared game-mode structs CssSubStruct and TrainingModeState, including PlayerInitData, HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, animation frame, menu value, and HSD_Text fields.
Refined fn_80160DE8 prototype from unknown params to void fn_80160DE8(HSD_JObj*, u8, s32, u8, f32, f32), enabling typed calls from gm_1832.

what_changed: Expanded gm_1832.c dependencies with many concrete includes: HSD aobj/cobj/dobj/mobj/wobj/gobjplink/sislib/random/runtime, gm_1601, gm_1A36, gmmain_lib, gm_1B03.static.h, ifstatus, ifall, it_266F, lb_0192, lblanguage, mn/types, and mnname.

Reworked static globals in gm_1832.c from broad padding into partially identified fields, including lbl_804735A8 JObj/timer fields and lbl_804735E8 splash-screen fields for character counts, ids, costumes, metal flags, SObj display GObj, and image descriptor indices.
Introduced local layout structs for ClassicSlotVals, ClassicCharLayout, ClassicTeamEntry, and ClassicSplashRow, each guarded with STATIC_ASSERT size checks.
Refined a large pointed-to data table lbl_804D6604 from raw padding into structured classic-mode layout arrays at offsets such as x00, x18, x3C, x6C, x37C, x57C, x630, x654, x678, and x6A8.
Added static data arrays and strings used by the decompiled code, including Japanese/US text bytes, character scale/offset data, and archive/resource names such as IrRdMap and IrNml.
Added CssSubStruct and TrainingModeState to gm/types.h, making later gm_1832 signatures such as gm_80189CDC(StartMeleeData*) return TrainingModeState* instead of UNK_RET.

Updated gm_1832.h prototypes for many functions from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures, including s32-returning status helpers, HSD_GObj* callbacks, HSD_JObj* animation helpers, HSD_Text* return, and void no-arg functions.
Updated gm_1601.h so fn_80160DE8 has a typed signature matching calls in gm_1832 that pass an HSD_JObj pointer, fighter id, flags, and two float scale/position parameters.
smart_moves: Converted unknown headers only as needed to support real callsites, especially fn_80160DE8, rather than leaving casts or UNK_PARAMS around new gm_1832 code.
Used STATIC_ASSERT on inferred asset/layout structs, which is a good safeguard when replacing raw padding with named fields in data-heavy game-mode code.
Kept uncertain fields offset-named, such as x6C, x37C, x57C, x6A8, pad_570, and pad_60C, avoiding overconfident semantic names while still exposing the layout needed for matching.
Modeled the classic splash/layout data as small reusable structs instead of one monolithic padding blob, making repeated expressions more readable and easier to audit against offsets.

Reused existing engine/library APIs directly, including HSD_JObj translation/scale helpers, HSD_TObjReqAnimAll, HSD_AObjSetRate, HSD_SisLib text functions, Player_GetEntity, Player_GetEntityAtIndex, lbAudioAx, gmMainLib, language checks, and name-text helpers.
Preserved decompilation progress even with fuzzy matches, with the author openly flagging that not many functions were 100% matched and that at least one issue involved data/offset/linking behavior.
decomp_lessons: For large game-mode decompilation PRs, typed prototypes in neighboring headers can be as important as the C body: one corrected helper signature can remove many casts and make callsite reconstruction clearer.

When asset tables drive positioning, scale, and text layout, replacing raw padding with offset-accurate structs plus STATIC_ASSERTs gives future decompilers a safer base even if field semantics remain unknown.
It is acceptable to land fuzzy-but-improving matches when the uncertainty is documented, especially in data/offset-heavy code where final exactness may depend on linking and table placement.
Do not over-name inferred game-mode data. Offset-style names with comments are preferable to guessed semantic names when evidence only proves layout and usage.

Splash/menu code often crosses many subsystems at once: HSD objects, SIS text, audio, language, player state, archive resources, and game-mode tables. Including exact subsystem headers can expose true signatures and reduce UNK usage.
Shared state structs such as TrainingModeState and CssSubStruct belong in gm/types.h when exposed through headers or used by multiple gm functions, rather than being kept as local anonymous blobs.
naming_conventions: Unmatched or partially understood functions remain address-based, e.g. fn_8018325C, fn_80184AB8, fn_80189B88, while lifecycle functions keep gm_80186DFC_OnFrame and gm_80186E30_OnEnter style names.
Inferred structs use descriptive but conservative names such as ClassicSlotVals, ClassicCharLayout, ClassicTeamEntry, ClassicSplashRow, ClassicCharData, CssSubStruct, and TrainingModeState.
Unknown fields retain offset-style names such as x00, x04, x08, x37C, x57C, x6A8, pad_14, pad_570, and pad_60C.
Header prototypes continue the project convention of address comments, e.g. /* 18504C */ void fn_8018504C(void);
Static data symbols retain original lbl_80xxxxxx names unless only a local C type or comment is inferred.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit stack padding markers such as PAD_STACK(8) and PAD_STACK(16) in functions where stack layout affected matching.
Introduced local temporaries such as scale_factor, x_adj, y_adj, and new_var to shape generated code around expected instruction order and float arithmetic.
Used casts to match signedness and width-sensitive comparisons, for example casting u8/u16 fields to s32 or u16 before comparisons against constants.

Modeled JObj transform copying through Vec3 temporaries and HSD_JObjGetTranslation/HSD_JObjSetTranslate/HSD_JObjGetScale/HSD_JObjSetScale calls, matching engine API patterns instead of raw field access.
Represented byte strings and float tables as static arrays in the translation unit to match data references for text and layout calculations.
Used bitfield-style updates through u8_bits casts in fn_8018A364 to express result flag writes while preserving byte/bit behavior.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the provided slice.
Author comment: 'Lots of fuzzy matches, not a lot of 100% ones, I still believe this is an improvement :)'
Author comment: 'The function that broke is data / offset stuff, will be resolved when linking'
PR body only said: 'Tracking PR as per usual'
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching gm_1832.c functions that were fuzzy in PR #2329, especially fn_80184AB8, fn_80186634, fn_801891F4, and fn_8018A364.
Search for subsequent renames or field refinements for ClassicSlotVals, ClassicCharLayout, ClassicTeamEntry, ClassicSplashRow, CssSubStruct, and TrainingModeState.
Check whether the data/offset linking issue mentioned by the author was resolved in a follow-up PR or by later linker/data placement changes.
Compare gm_1832.h after later PRs to see which UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations remained and which were further typed.
Search for later uses of the typed fn_80160DE8(HSD_JObj*, u8, s32, u8, f32, f32) signature.
